code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a mean of transportation into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple phrase templates for common predicates
    templates = {
        "alternativeName": "{subject} is also known as {object}.",
        "bodyStyle": "{subject} has a {object} body style.",
        "engine": "{subject} is powered by {object}.",
        "manufacturer": "{subject} was manufactured by {object}.",
        "relatedMeanOfTransportation": "{subject} is related to {object}.",
        "transmission": "{subject} features a {object} transmission.",
        "wheelbase": "{subject} has a wheelbase of {object} inches.",
        "builder": "{subject} was built by {object}.",
        "completionDate": "{subject} was completed on {object}.",
        "length": "{subject} measures {object} millimetres in length.",
        "powerType": "{subject} uses {object} as its power type.",
        "shipClass": "{subject} belongs to the {object} class.",
        "shipDisplacement": "{subject} displaces {object} tonnes.",
        "shipLaunch": "{subject} was launched on {object}.",
        "shipOrdered": "{subject} was ordered on {object}.",
        "shipPower": "{subject} is powered by {object}.",
        "topSpeed": "{subject} can reach a top speed of {object}.",
        "location": "{subject} is located in {object}.",
        "christeningDate": "{subject} was christened on {object}.",
        "maidenVoyage": "{subject} made its maiden voyage on {object}.",
        "owner": "{subject} is owned by {object}.",
        "shipBeam": "{subject} has a beam of {object} metres.",
        "shipInService": "{subject} entered service on {object}.",
        "status": "{subject} is currently {object}.",
        "activeYearsStartDate": "{subject} started active years on {object}.",
        "shipLaidDown": "{subject} was laid down on {object}.",
        "buildDate": "{subject} was built between {object}.",
        "cylinderCount": "{subject} has {object} cylinders.",
        "totalProduction": "{subject} produced a total of {object} units.",
        "countryOrigin": "{subject} originated from {object}.",
        "diameter": "{subject} has a diameter of {object} metres.",
        "failedLaunches": "{subject} experienced {object} failed launch(es).",
        "rocketStages": "{subject} has {object} rocket stage(s).",
        "totalLaunches": "{subject} performed {object} launch(es).",
        # generic fallback
        "default": "{subject} {predicate} {object}."
    }

    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate, templates["default"])
        clause = tmpl.format(
            subject=triple.subject,
            predicate=triple.predicate.replace("_", " "),
            object=triple.object
        )
        # Ensure clause ends with a period
        clause = clause.rstrip()
        if not clause.endswith('.'):
            clause += '.'
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine clauses into a single fluent paragraph
    # Use commas for early clauses and "and" before the last one
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        return clauses[0]
    else:
        return " ".join(clauses)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
